card.intuition: Managing and provisioning computer data centers through machine-readable definition files (code), rather than physical hardware configuration or interactive configuration tools.
card.analogy: Ordering a kit car: instead of going to a factory and manually welding parts together (Manual AWS Console clicking), you have a blueprint (Terraform file). You give the blueprint to a robot, and it builds the exact car you described perfectly every time.
when_to_use: Scaling your infrastructure to multiple regions identically
Keeping track of infrastructure changes in Git (Version Control)
Automating the setup of dev/staging environments
gotchas: State Management: tools like Terraform need a 'State File' to remember what they built. If you lose this file, the tool won't know how to update your production servers.
Manual changes: once you use IaC, you must STOP clicking buttons in the AWS/Azure console, or your code will get out of sync with reality ('State Drift').

exercise.prompt: Identify the most popular cloud-agnostic IaC tool used to define resources using HCL (HashiCorp Configuration Language).
exercise.test_code:
def check(ans): return ans.lower() == "terraform"
assert check("Terraform")
print("PASS")
